Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. William Blake, Remote by the Sea 48 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Agents of Chaos 57 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Researchers find 3,500-year-old loom that reveals textile revolution 64 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Cherri – programming language that compiles to an Apple Shortuct 236 upvotes, 46 comments
  5. Fedware: Government apps that spy harder than the apps they ban 362 upvotes, 113 comments
  6. Do your own writing 321 upvotes, 100 comments
  7. The ladder is missing rungs – Engineering Progression When AI Ate the Middle 69 upvotes, 17 comments
  8. How to turn anything into a router 564 upvotes, 204 comments
  9. Roulette Computers: Hidden Devices That Predict Spins 55 upvotes, 15 comments
  10. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 190 upvotes, 75 comments
  11. Recover Apple Keychain 51 upvotes, 15 comments
  12. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 114 upvotes, 45 comments
  13. Seeing Like a Spreadsheet 64 upvotes, 23 comments
  14. Turning a MacBook into a touchscreen with $1 of hardware (2018) 165 upvotes, 74 comments
  15. FTC action against Match and OkCupid for deceiving users, sharing personal data 241 upvotes, 116 comments
  16. A sea of sparks: Seeing radioactivity 46 upvotes, 16 comments
  17. Take better notes, by hand 167 upvotes, 79 comments
  18. OpenGridWorks: The Electricity Infrasctructure, Mapped 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Show HN: Coasts – Containerized Hosts for Agents 52 upvotes, 20 comments
  20. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 294 upvotes, 170 comments
  21. Bird brains (2023) 287 upvotes, 181 comments
  22. Why I'm betting on ATProto (and why you should, too) 32 upvotes, 13 comments
  23. The Digital Leviathan 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. Learn Claude Code by doing, not reading 113 upvotes, 71 comments
  25. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 248 upvotes, 194 comments
  26. IronGlass Brings Legendary Soviet Cinema Lenses to Mirrorless Cameras 17 upvotes, 7 comments
  27. Android Developer Verification 90 upvotes, 67 comments
  28. In math, rigor is vital, but are digitized proofs taking it too far? 98 upvotes, 84 comments
  29. From Proxmox to FreeBSD and Sylve in our office lab 74 upvotes, 64 comments
  30. Apple pulls the plug on its high-priced, oft-neglected Mac Pro desktop 5 upvotes, 2 comments

Last generated 8:30am, 31 March 2026. Source here.
